target_roles.title: Product Manager
target_roles.why: I've always been the person in the room asking 'but why does the user need this?' and 'what are we really solving?' The PM role is where my technical instincts and my love for ideation and collaboration actually intersect. I've been doing PM work as an SDE — just without the title.

target_roles.title: Senior Software Engineer
target_roles.why: I have strong full-stack and AI engineering experience. If I'm in an SDE role, I want it to be one where I'm close to product decisions and user impact — not just translating requirements into code.

career_narrative: I started as a software engineer in enterprise analytics, building backend systems, AI reporting pipelines, and running large-scale migrations. But what I realized along the way was that the moments I was most energized weren't when I was writing code — they were when I was questioning requirements, designing flows, pushing back on what didn't serve users, and collaborating cross-team to ship something that actually mattered. I've led migrations that saved $2M+, built AI tools that reduced workflows from 8 clicks to 2, and consistently been recognized as a change agent at my company. Now I want to own the 'why' behind what gets built — and that means product management.

key_achievements.title: DAX to SQL Migration — $2M+ Cost Reduction
key_achievements.description: Led the migration of Power BI's DAX-based reporting to SQL on Azure Databricks Delta Tables. Wrote complex CTEs, running totals, cumulative sums, and conditional column logic. Built a centralized migration and onboarding repo (50+ scripts) that is now the base for all production onboarding. Migrated 180+ customers on UAT. Estimated $2M+ annual savings from BI subscription reduction.
key_achievements.impact: $2M+ annual cost reduction, 180+ domains, 50+ reusable scripts

key_achievements.title: AI-Driven Reporting System (RAG + FastAPI)
key_achievements.description: Built an AI reporting pipeline using RAG with FastAPI, OpenAI, and VectorDB. Supported 24+ visualization types and 20+ data transformation operations. Reduced consultant workflows from 8+ clicks to 2 per operation for report search, generation, and email triggers. Delivered multiple prototypes under tight deadlines for ad-hoc client demos.
key_achievements.impact: 8+ to 2 clicks per workflow, 24+ viz types, 20+ data ops

key_achievements.title: Enterprise Incident Resolution
key_achievements.description: Resolved 70+ production incidents for 20+ enterprise clients within 3 months, while simultaneously fixing 20+ UI bugs from UX reviews.

key_achievements.title: Graphical Password Authenticator — Techathon 2nd Place
key_achievements.description: Built and published a React-based graphical password authenticator as an NPM package. Won 2nd place in internal company Techathon.

key_achievements.title: GEP Pulse — In-House Radio Initiative
key_achievements.description: Initiated and launched GEP Pulse, an in-house radio channel for music and office news, as part of the GEP Funcoholics community.

recognitions: Change Agents Q1&Q2 2025 — DBSQL solution and client onboarding contributions
Catalyst Q3 2024 — AI deliverables for urgent demos, high-impact results
SPOT Recognition Oct 2024 — UI version upgrades, proactive coordination
Change Agents Q2 2024 — AI scope adaptation, SLA compliance, client impact

personality.tone: warm, direct, specific — not corporate or stiff
traits: initiator — starts things, not just executes them
boundary-aware — firm on work-life balance without apology
collaborative — naturally says 'we', thinks in teams
curious about user experience and product decisions
values: ships things that matter to real users
red_flags_in_companies: glorifying overwork or expecting beyond-hours commitment as default
pure maintenance/support roles with no product influence
heavy internal politics / top-down decision making
no user feedback loops or experimentation culture

cover_letter_structure: Open with a specific observation about the company/product/problem — not 'I am writing to apply'
Connect their challenge or mission to something you've genuinely thought about
One concrete story — a specific moment where you showed the relevant skill with real numbers
Why this role, this company, this moment in time — make it specific
Close with genuine curiosity, not 'I look forward to hearing from you'
recruiter_reply_structure: Acknowledge the opportunity specifically — show you read it
Quick, confident positioning — who you are and what you bring
One relevant hook that makes you memorable
language_rules: Use 'I' confidently — no excessive hedging or softening
Specific > general at all times — name the technology, the metric, the decision
No buzzword soup — no 'synergy', 'leverage', 'passionate about innovation'
If you use a claim, back it with a number or a story
what_makes_her_different: She has shipped AI features (RAG, FastAPI, OpenAI) before it was mainstream at most Indian enterprises
She thinks in user flows and experience, not just code
She has the rare combination of deep backend/data eng + AI + product instincts
She initiates things — Pulse, shared repos, cross-team alignment — without being asked
She has operated with enterprise-scale responsibility early in career (NIT + GEP enterprise clients)

certifications: Blockchain Specialization — University at Buffalo & SUNY (Coursera, Jan 2023)
